title: Trump threatens to destroy bridges, power plants, and nuclear sites in Iran if his ships are attacked
summary: The US president warns he will attack Iranian infrastructure, while the Pentagon estimates a war would cost $6 trillion.

title: Alphabet quadruples its quarterly net profit thanks to cloud and AI
summary: Google's parent company reported a net profit of 18 trillion yen in the second quarter, four times more than the previous year, driven by the growth of its cloud business and artificial intelligence.

title: Extreme heat alert in central Japan with temperatures exceeding 40 degrees
summary: Weather authorities warn of the risk of heatstroke with temperatures of up to 40°C or more in cities such as Nagoya, Kyoto, and Gifu.

title: US signs civilian nuclear agreement with Saudi Arabia allowing uranium enrichment
summary: The pact, which seeks to counter China and Russia, authorizes Riyadh to enrich uranium, raising concerns about nuclear proliferation.

title: Houthis attack two Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea for defying blockade
summary: The Yemeni rebel group attacks ships that, it claims, violated the maritime blockade imposed by the Saudi-led coalition.

title: JAXA successfully completes combustion test of the Epsilon S rocket engine
summary: The Japanese space agency successfully conducts the ignition test of the new solid-fuel rocket, whose launch is scheduled for fiscal year 2026.

title: House fire in Osaka leaves one dead and one missing
summary: A fire destroys a wooden house in Katano, Osaka; firefighters find a body and the whereabouts of the occupant's sister are unknown.

title: Japanese supermarkets face increase in thefts at self-checkout counters
summary: Thefts at self-checkout counters generate millions in losses, but they are difficult to detect and report due to the difficulty of distinguishing between errors and intentional crimes.

title: Campaign for Nagano governorship kicks off; incumbent governor seeks fifth term
summary: The incumbent governor and a former Upper House member are running as independent candidates for the August 9 election.

title: Yen falls to 163 yen per dollar and Finance Minister issues warning
summary: The Japanese currency weakens to 163 yen against the dollar, its lowest level in months, prompting a verbal intervention from the Finance Minister.

title: Trial concludes in death of Wishma at immigration center in Japan
summary: The court will decide on December 11 whether there was medical negligence in the case of the Sri Lankan woman who died in custody.

title: Japanese prime minister denies involvement in defamatory video against electoral rival
summary: The prime minister presents an affidavit from his secretary to prove he was not involved in the dissemination of a video attacking an opponent, but the opposition criticizes the explanation.

title: Nikkei 225 index recovers strongly driven by tech rally in US
summary: The Nikkei 225 rises more than 700 points to surpass 66,800, boosted by the rise in semiconductor stocks in the United States and the increase in South Korea.
